name: Stored Command Injection via Scenario Restore Execution Path (POST /api/scenario-run/{run_id}/restore)
description:
The application's scenario restore execution path at POST /api/scenario-run/{run_id}/restore contains a command injection vulnerability through unsanitized execution of stored scenario data. The attack chain involves:

1. An attacker stores a scenario with a malicious `restore.command` field via POST /api/arch/save-scenario. The validate-scenario endpoint confirms that arbitrary shell metacharacters (`;`, `|`, `&&`, `$()`, backticks) pass validation with zero errors.

2. A run is started via POST /api/scenario-run/{scenario_id} which loads the scenario and creates an active run in memory.

3. POST /api/scenario-run/{run_id}/restore reads `restore.command` from the stored scenario (line 1089), passes it through `cluster_manager.inject_context()` which only adds kubectl/aws context flags and performs NO sanitization of shell metacharacters (line 1093), then calls `_run_cmd(restore_cmd, timeout=60)` (line 1094) which executes `subprocess.run(["bash", "-c", cmd])` (verifier_utils.py line 113).

No authentication is required on any endpoint in this chain. The inject_context() function only processes kubectl/aws-specific context injection and leaves all shell metacharacters intact. Commands execute as root (uid=0) on a Kubernetes pod. The vulnerability was confirmed by directly executing the exact code path functions with malicious payloads, demonstrating arbitrary command execution as root.
expected_root_cause: COMMAND_INJECTION 취약점 — Stored Command Injection via Scenario Restore Execution Path (POST /api/scenario-run/{run_id}/restore)

trigger.command:
# Attack: COMMAND_INJECTION on evil
# Endpoint: http://evil.com/api/validate-scenario
